109643.explanation: The Barendrecht executive board has four aldermen and a mayor. In its composition, EVB considers quality, trust, and suitability decisive. Not a fixed quota, but the best people in the right place.

109645.explanation: EVB is open to residents taking over the management of a public park. For facilities such as a sports hall, that is not realistic. This requires tailor-made solutions for each situation.

109647.explanation: Completely free opening hours can disproportionately affect livability. In areas such as the old village, protecting residential quality is necessary. EVB chooses clear rules and balance between hospitality industry and residents.

109648.explanation: There are already national schemes for joint sustainable energy projects. EVB believes the municipality does not need to add extra subsidies on top of this.

109650.explanation: We already have an asylum seekers center on Zuider Carnisseweg.

109652.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves can remain allowed, but EVB believes that burning, especially in residential areas, must be done responsibly, with attention to health and inconvenience for neighbors.

109654.explanation: The fireworks show in Rotterdam is nearby.

109657.explanation: For people who cannot afford it, there is a scheme in Barendrecht.

109662.explanation: EVB wants a total of 10 neighborhood police officers and 10 enforcement officers (boa’s) in Barendrecht.

109663.explanation: But with good consideration for privacy.

109664.explanation: EVB considers shelter for domestic violence victims essential, but having its own shelter is not an end in itself. It is about immediate safety and good assistance. If regional shelter offers that better and faster, EVB chooses that.

109665.explanation: Enforcement officers increasingly work in difficult and sometimes threatening situations. A baton can contribute to their own safety and to de-escalation, provided they are well trained, clearly regulated, and part of a broader safety approach.

109666.explanation: EVB believes that starters are mainly helped by more affordable homes and fewer rules. A starter loan increases price pressure and ultimately mostly helps the market, not the starter themselves. Moreover, the municipality is not a bank.

109670.explanation: We welcome converting vacant offices into housing, but no municipal subsidy is needed for that. EVB opts for clear rules and room for initiative, not extra subsidies.

109672.explanation: Those receiving social assistance benefits also provide something in return where possible. This already happens in Barendrecht: socially useful work helps people stay active and increases their chances on the job market.

109675.explanation: There are already many. And people should be able to charge at home more easily themselves.

109677.explanation: We are against generic paid parking.

109678.explanation: An environmental zone suits large cities with heavy traffic pressure. For Barendrecht, that is not the case. EVB chooses realistic measures that fit the scale and character of our municipality.

109679.explanation: Where possible, people should be able to choose care that fits their religion, culture, or beliefs. That is already the starting point in Barendrecht. Only in emergency care is that choice not always possible.

109681.explanation: Free provision for everyone is not necessary. In Barendrecht, people with low incomes are already supported. EVB believes assistance should be focused on those who need it.

109682.explanation: EVB does not want to lower the property tax if that comes at the expense of facilities. Taxation must be fair and remain in line with the regional average: no higher than necessary, with attention to affordability and our good facilities.

109685.explanation: In Barendrecht there is hardly any space to further expand industrial sites. EVB chooses to handle the scarce space carefully and prefers to focus on better utilization and sustainability of existing sites.

109687.explanation: EVB believes that scarce social rental homes are primarily intended for residents of Barendrecht. Priority for status holders does not fit here; everyone must compete fairly under the same rules.

109689.explanation: EVB supports solar panels on roofs and built-up areas, not on agricultural land. The open green space is scarce and must be preserved for nature and food production.

109690.explanation: The municipality should help sports clubs renew their facilities.

109691.explanation: EVB recognizes the importance of school swimming lessons, but in practice, schools do not want to return to it. Allocating money without support from schools is therefore not useful.

109694.explanation: Barendrecht should remain Barendrecht. EVB therefore wants high-rise flats only where it makes sense, such as in the new Stationstuinen district, and not in existing neighborhoods.

109696.explanation: EVB supports local meeting places. Neighborhood centers in the districts strengthen cohesion, make support easily accessible, and ensure that residents can continue to meet each other close to home.

109700.explanation: Free establishment applies in the Netherlands, also for a mosque. Anyone who wants to deviate from the zoning plan or other regulations must apply for a permit. The municipality assesses this objectively, just like any other application.

109702.explanation: More meeting places for young people are needed. EVB focuses on low-threshold, safe places in neighborhoods where young people can meet and be supported by good youth work.

109648.explanation: We support assistance in setting up a cooperative or legal entity and developing a plan.

109650.explanation: We meet the requirements of the Distribution Act.

109652.explanation: In any case, a ban on burning during unfavorable weather conditions.

109657.explanation: We support the existing minimum welfare scheme that allows this.

109662.explanation: There are currently 11 municipal enforcement officers (boa's). We consider that sufficient for the current situation.

109664.explanation: We want a preventive and active policy to prevent abuse of women and children. We cooperate regionally.

109670.explanation: The municipality pursues an active policy to initiate projects.

109672.explanation: This obligation is being abolished by the national government. It is counterproductive. Within the context of labor (re)integration, people perform meaningful social tasks that suit them.

109675.explanation: If there is a need for more charging points, the number of places will be expanded.

109677.explanation: The annual increase of 350 cars requires measures in the coming years, including neighborhoods with parking permits.

109679.explanation: This is taken into account as much as possible but is not an absolute right.

109681.explanation: We want to expand the existing scheme for menstrual poverty.

109682.explanation: The revenue from the property tax is important to keep Barendrecht social and green.

109685.explanation: There is no room for expansions: no land and other priorities, such as housing construction.

109687.explanation: We have a task to house status holders.

109689.explanation: No agricultural land is available anymore in Barendrecht.

109690.explanation: Primarily a task for BVV itself. The municipality assists with a one-third scheme.

109691.explanation: Through the minimum welfare scheme, a reimbursement for swimming lessons can be obtained.

109694.explanation: High residential buildings will be built in Stationstuinen. In existing neighborhoods, we allow high-rise buildings, or higher than usual, if possible.

109696.explanation: We are in favor of involving sports and cultural clubs, churches, and community real estate as neighborhood centers.

109702.explanation: We want a study to determine whether current facilities suffice or if other choices need to be made.

109643.explanation: The SGP-Christian Union views men and women as equal. However, equality is not achieved through quotas. In politics, capacities should be decisive. Moreover, equal distribution requires an even number of college members, which unnecessarily complicates forming the college.

109645.explanation: The SGP-Christian Union supports, under conditions, the self-management of, for example, a sports hall or public green space. It fosters a sense of shared responsibility and offers a great opportunity to get to know and collaborate with fellow villagers.

109647.explanation: Timely closing of hospitality venues contributes to the health, livability, and residential pleasure of local residents. Hospitality owners have the freedom to close earlier. Reasons for regulating opening hours include public order, preventing nuisance and crowding for residents, and safety.

109648.explanation: Sustainability and good stewardship of creation are important to the SGP-Christian Union. Citizens should be encouraged to take their own responsibility. The municipality facilitates the use of national financial arrangements. We want to be cautious with structural subsidies.

109650.explanation: Based on the national distribution law, Barendrecht must accommodate asylum seekers. The possible establishment of a reception center must be well coordinated with the surroundings and the accommodation should be humane, safe, and sober. At this moment, it has not been sufficiently demonstrated that the Southern Randpark is the right location.

109652.explanation: Wood stoves bring coziness to the home. Unfortunately, wood burning can also contribute to air pollution. Therefore, the municipality encourages environmentally friendly burning behavior. We are mindful of the environment and neighbors who may suffer from 'bad' burning habits.

109654.explanation: During the New Year's celebrations, the municipality facilitates a fireworks show for and by the community, for example through neighborhood associations.

109657.explanation: Sports are healthy and fun. However, there are also other good forms of leisure activities. Therefore, low-income households receive a financial allowance to be spent at their discretion, which they can also use for art or culture.

109662.explanation: BOAs (municipal enforcement officers) are important for maintaining safety, connecting with neighborhoods and youth, and can also perform preventive work. In addition to more BOAs, the SGP-ChristianUnion also wants more neighborhood police officers and a dedicated police station in Barendrecht.

109663.explanation: Maintaining safety is an important task of the municipality. In cases of front door explosions, robberies, etc., the placement of temporary mobile cameras is already an option. We want to guard against a surveillance society. The collected data should not be kept longer than necessary.

109664.explanation: Violence against women is a major problem. A shelter for women escaping violence can help provide an escape route and thus prevent violent escalation. As far as we are concerned, the municipality of Barendrecht organizes this on a regional basis.

109665.explanation: The SGP-ChristianUnion believes that BOAs need proper equipment. The allocation of these means of force should depend on the likelihood of violence.

109666.explanation: To improve the affordability of owner-occupied homes for starters, the SGP-ChristianUnion supports the continuation of affordability arrangements, such as the starters' loan.

109670.explanation: Creative solutions are needed to tackle the housing shortage. Converting offices is a good idea. The municipality can facilitate this. As the SGP-ChristianUnion, we believe that project developers should bear the financial risks involved.

109672.explanation: It is good to give back to society. Of course, consideration is given to each person's capabilities. People who cannot work should not be forced to do so.

109675.explanation: Electric driving can help reduce environmental pollution. Good facilities need to be in place for this. According to the SGP-ChristianUnion, the municipality should also focus on stimulating the use of public transport and cycling.

109677.explanation: The SGP-ChristianUnion opposes paid parking or similar schemes. Parking nuisance should be addressed in other ways.

109678.explanation: Banning certain vehicles from the center does not fit the village-like and open culture we want in Barendrecht. We focus on positive measures that encourage cycling and use of public transport.

109679.explanation: If you need care, it is important that the caregiver connects well with, and understands, your worldview and beliefs.

109681.explanation: Some women and girls dread their menstruation or cannot participate fully because they cannot afford tampons or sanitary pads. The municipality of Barendrecht offers these products free and easily accessible, for example in public restrooms or secondary schools.

109682.explanation: Homeowners should not be a cash cow for the municipality. If the municipality needs money, it should first look for possible budget cuts.

109685.explanation: If necessary, companies must be able to expand. We first look at vacant spaces within business parks, and then at possible new locations. However, there must also remain space for housing, and vacancy in business parks must be prevented.

109687.explanation: Status holders should quickly get their own home. That is good for integration. At the same time, we find it unfair if 'regular' starters have to wait years because status holders always take precedence. The municipality retains policy discretion in this regard.

109689.explanation: Solar energy is one of the energy sources of the future. Solar panels on agricultural land can help. However, from a stewardship perspective, we must also use fertile agricultural land sparingly.

109690.explanation: BVV is responsible for its own accommodation. We find it good that the municipality of Barendrecht provides financial support. In the current plans, in our opinion, too great a demand is placed on the municipality's financial resources.

109691.explanation: In a water-rich municipality like Barendrecht, it is important that all children can swim. School swimming is a good tool for this. The SGP-ChristianUnion primarily sees swimming lessons as a task for parents. For low-income families who cannot afford this, arrangements for swimming lessons are already possible.

109694.explanation: The SGP-ChristianUnion finds it important to maintain the village character of Barendrecht. An exception applies to the Station Gardens area.

109696.explanation: In the opinion of the SGP-ChristianUnion, it is not strictly necessary for every neighborhood to have its own community center. However, it is important that the existing meeting places are sufficiently accessible.

109700.explanation: The SGP-ChristianUnion supports freedom of religion. This does not mean that establishing churches or mosques should always be allowed. The municipality must make an honest assessment in each case, including the suitability of the location, and guard against discrimination based on religion.

109702.explanation: The SGP-ChristianUnion is warm towards youth work. Meeting and recreation for young people, within a safe environment, benefits the community later on! It is also important that meeting places fit the neighborhood and do not cause nuisance.

109643.explanation: The CDA Barendrecht wants good aldermen. That could mean that there are only female aldermen, only male aldermen, or a combination of both.

109645.explanation: Volunteers and associations are the backbone of our society. If something can be solved without the municipality doing it, then we welcome that, of course under the municipality's supervision.

109647.explanation: Possible opening hours are already set broadly enough. We leave it up to the cafés and restaurants themselves to fill these in within these hours.

109648.explanation: To do this, the municipality must be cooperative and indicate what is possible and what is not. Furthermore, the CDA Barendrecht believes that this should not remain mere advice. Actual support must also be among the options.

109650.explanation: We already have an emergency location in Barendrecht. The CDA Barendrecht prefers this location be expanded into an asylum seekers center rather than creating a new location.

109652.explanation: However, the emissions caused by fireplaces are irresponsible. We do not want to take them away from residents, but we do believe that within a few years a flue gas filter should be installed. This will reduce the emission of harmful substances to a minimum.

109654.explanation: There is now a national ban on setting off fireworks. Organizing a fireworks show costs taxpayers a lot of money. That money could be better spent than on a few minutes of fireworks.

109657.explanation: This is already included in the existing minimum income policy.

109662.explanation: Very necessary in Barendrecht. The CDA Barendrecht also supports better equipping current BOAs because society is becoming tougher and more is demanded from these BOAs.

109663.explanation: Camera surveillance ensures more safety. To prevent disturbances, violations, and crimes, but also as a means to resolve cases. Moreover, this is necessary to guarantee the safety of all citizens.

109664.explanation: The CDA Barendrecht is a strong supporter of a women's shelter in Barendrecht. Violence behind closed doors is a bigger problem than we often think. If we want to do something about this, there must also be an option to temporarily offer residents a safe place.

109665.explanation: Aggression against enforcers and service providers has increased sharply in recent years. Equipping them with a baton for protection is almost indispensable in today's society.

109666.explanation: Starters' loans are provided by lenders and not by the government.

109670.explanation: The CDA Barendrecht supports this. However, it is up to the owner of a property to do this in a financially feasible way. Generally, this works out well.

109672.explanation: The CDA Barendrecht believes that we may expect something in return. It also ensures that people remain active and increases the chances of exiting social assistance.

109675.explanation: There is currently a shortage of charging stations. The rules regarding charging your own vehicle near your home should also be relaxed.

109677.explanation: Only if it is free for residents.

109678.explanation: The number of polluting vehicles has rapidly decreased in recent years. The expectation is that this will continue, and therefore we see no need for such environmental zones.

109679.explanation: It concerns the care that is provided and paid for, with corresponding reimbursements. There is no room for such choices that would make care more expensive. This also does not fit with the path towards an inclusive society.

109681.explanation: This support can be obtained within the social minimum policy and from the poverty fund.

109682.explanation: To be reviewed annually based on the increase in the value of properties.

109685.explanation: We are already familiar with Nieuw Reijerwaard. In addition, there are several other areas within the municipality that can be designated as business parks.

109687.explanation: The municipality is already responsible for housing status holders, and this goes beyond the regular allocation through the housing cooperatives.

109689.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities for the municipality to install solar panels along roads, dikes, noise barriers, etc. We do not need to sacrifice agricultural land for this.

109690.explanation: But only partially; the club itself must raise the majority of the finances.

109691.explanation: The safety of children is paramount. Experience shows that if this is not taught at school, it will never happen.

109694.explanation: Only in the Stationstuinen neighborhood and under strict conditions.

109696.explanation: We believe there is too much lack of neighborhood and community feeling, causing people to live alongside each other. A community center within walking distance contributes to a sense of togetherness.

109700.explanation: Freedom of religion is for all of us, as arranged in the constitution.

109702.explanation: Too few meeting places means young people are more likely to hang out on the streets.

109643.explanation: Quality and competence take precedence over quotas. Aim for diversity through merit-based selection, not mandatory 50/50 distribution.

109647.explanation: Give entrepreneurs space as long as this does not cause nuisance. Enforce regulations on noise, safety, and livability.

109648.explanation: The municipality is only a facilitator; make it easy, prevent bureaucracy, and avoid unprofitable subsidies.

109650.explanation: Barendrecht takes its legal responsibility, provided it is manageable: distribution, safety, integration, and clear agreements with the national government and residents.

109652.explanation: Freedom, but with responsibility: allow wood burning, discourage it in case of nuisance, and promote clean technologies and information.

109654.explanation: Municipal fireworks displays are expensive and risky. Focus on safety and enforcement; possibly facilitate a regional show without extra costs or an alternative.

109657.explanation: Participation is important: sports promote health and integration. Provide targeted support through existing poverty schemes, also for other groups, with easy access.

109662.explanation: More community enforcement officers increase visibility and enforcement. Safety and livability require capacity and clear priorities.

109663.explanation: Camera surveillance helps against nuisance and crime. Apply it targetedly, with privacy frameworks, evaluation, and deployment at hotspots.

109664.explanation: Protection against domestic violence is a core task. A shelter prevents escalation and increases safety. Preference is for a regional approach.

109665.explanation: Community enforcement officers must be able to work safely. Provide batons with strict training, proportionality, and supervision.

109666.explanation: Starter loans increase opportunities for young buyers.

109670.explanation: The VVD supports converting vacant offices into housing; provided there is a viable plan underlying it. It remains the entrepreneur’s responsibility.

109672.explanation: Reciprocity: those who can, contribute. Social work helps with rhythm, skills, and outflow to paid work.

109675.explanation: Charging infrastructure is necessary for the energy transition. Designate charging points but balance with parking capacity and grid capacity.

109677.explanation: Parking permits only where truly needed. Prevent extra rules and costs for Barendrecht residents.

109678.explanation: An environmental zone affects residents and entrepreneurs. Choose stimulation, cleaner mobility, and enforcement of existing standards.

109679.explanation: Care recipients get freedom of choice for a suitable care provider, as long as this is feasible and does not lead to exclusion or discrimination.

109681.explanation: Addressing period poverty is basic dignity. Provide free products via schools and neighborhood points, targeted at those who need them.

109682.explanation: Lowering property tax keeps housing affordable. First be critical of expenditures and efficiency; reduce burdens where possible.

109685.explanation: Expand business parks where appropriate: space for entrepreneurship and jobs. Combine with good accessibility and sustainability requirements.

109687.explanation: No priority: fairness for all home seekers. Status holders follow the regular waiting time; focus on extra building and faster procedures.

109689.explanation: Allow solar parks on agricultural land, with landscape integration and combination with nature/agricultural use; this accelerates sustainable energy.

109690.explanation: Large investments in BVV Barendrecht are not a core task. Priority lies with amateur sports, maintenance of existing facilities, and financial prudence.

109691.explanation: School swimming increases safety and equal opportunities. Investing prevents drownings and supports families.

109694.explanation: More high-rise buildings can ease the housing shortage, preferably near public transport and amenities. Ensure quality, parking, and appropriate streetscape.

109696.explanation: Strengthen existing neighborhood centers together. Invest where there is need and utilize multifunctional locations; prevent fragmentation.

109700.explanation: Freedom of religion: allow within laws and spatial regulations. Ensure safety, parking, and good integration into the neighborhood.

109702.explanation: Meeting places help with prevention and well-being. Create safe, managed spaces in consultation with the neighborhood and youth.

109643.explanation: A balanced distribution ensures better decisions and more recognition for residents.

109645.explanation: Those who volunteer for the neighborhood should have space and trust to do so.

109647.explanation: Unless this demonstrably leads to nuisance or safety problems.

109648.explanation: Local energy projects help speed up sustainability and connect residents.

109650.explanation: Barendrecht must also contribute to reception, with attention to scale, support, and good guidance.

109652.explanation: Permission can be granted as long as Barendrecht focuses on education, discouraging nuisance, and protecting residents.

109654.explanation: A central show is safer and causes less nuisance.

109657.explanation: Sports help people stay healthy and engaged.

109662.explanation: Visible enforcement officers create a safer feeling.

109663.explanation: In Barendrecht, camera surveillance may only be used if it is necessary and proportionate; privacy is the starting point.

109664.explanation: Victims of violence must be able to find safety quickly.

109666.explanation: It helps first-time buyers purchase their first home.

109670.explanation: Targeted subsidies make reuse cheaper and faster than new construction.

109672.explanation: Trust and guidance work better than coercion.

109675.explanation: That makes electric driving easier and more accessible.

109677.explanation: In Barendrecht residential areas, 30 km/h increases traffic safety; main and ring roads remain exceptions.

109678.explanation: A low-emission zone is not proportionate for Barendrecht given the limited traffic pressure and air quality issues.

109679.explanation: Care works better when it fits the person.

109689.explanation: Use roofs and industrial areas first.

109691.explanation: Water safety is essential for children.

109694.explanation: Building upwards prevents expansion into green areas.

109696.explanation: The municipality must invest in low-threshold meeting places where needed.

109700.explanation: Freedom of religion applies to everyone.

109702.explanation: Targeted meeting places, such as sports and skate facilities, work preventively against nuisance.

109643.explanation: Quality and engagement in Barendrecht are decisive, not gender. A council must be formed based on knowledge, experience, and residents' trust.

109645.explanation: If residents want to take over management themselves and can demonstrably do so responsibly, they should be given the chance. This increases involvement, strengthens community spirit, and can be more efficient than municipal management.

109647.explanation: Unlimited opening hours can lead to nuisance for neighbors. Clear municipal closing times provide peace, safety, and a good balance between hospitality venues and residential areas. Tailored solutions can be arranged via permits, not through complete freedom.

109648.explanation: Local initiatives arising from citizen power deserve support. The condition is that they are feasible, have support, and do not lead to extra burdens for residents.

109650.explanation: Asylum seeker centers can lead to nuisance, tensions, pressure on neighborhoods, housing supply, infrastructure, and facilities. The national government has not organized asylum policy properly and shifts problems onto municipalities. We reject the compulsion of the Distribution Act. Asylum centers come at the expense of our residents.

109652.explanation: A general ban is not necessary. However, attention must be paid to nuisance and health, focusing on education and enforcement in cases of excess.

109654.explanation: A central show increases safety and reduces nuisance, while residents can celebrate New Year's Eve together.

109657.explanation: Sports are important, but the municipality does not need to pay for memberships. There are already schemes and funds for people with low incomes. Additionally, outdoor sports, playing, and moving in public spaces are accessible and affordable alternatives.

109662.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and livability in the neighborhoods. More community officers ensure supervision, approachability, and faster tackling of nuisance and (minor) crime.

109663.explanation: Barendrechts Belang does not want a municipality with local surveillance. Therefore, cameras are only used where they demonstrably contribute to safety and with respect for privacy.

109664.explanation: Violence in the private sphere must be tackled decisively. A dedicated women's shelter is not necessarily needed, as this can also be organized regionally, where specialized facilities and expertise already exist.

109665.explanation: BOAs must be able to do their work safely, provided they are well trained and operate under clear rules.

109666.explanation: Buying a house is almost impossible for starters due to high prices and strict requirements. A starter loan can make just the difference and helps young residents of Barendrecht to keep living in their own municipality.

109670.explanation: The housing shortage is so severe that all permitted and reasonable instruments must be used to increase housing opportunities, including converting vacant real estate.

109672.explanation: Participation according to one's abilities strengthens self-worth and society. It is important to prevent labor displacement or exploitation.

109675.explanation: Barendrechts Belang has taken the initiative to realize more charging points (VPA's). More parking spaces with charging points make sustainable driving more accessible for residents and entrepreneurs.

109677.explanation: Barendrecht is used by many outsiders as a free parking lot. Parking permits can help reduce parking pressure and preserve space for local residents.

109678.explanation: An environmental zone disproportionately affects residents and entrepreneurs, while its impact is limited. This does not fit the village-like and accessible character of Barendrecht.

109679.explanation: People should be able to choose a care provider who suits them. Moreover, this is already established as a patient right in the Medical Treatment Agreement Act (WGBO).

109681.explanation: The municipality does not need to structurally provide tampons and sanitary pads. Assistance can be more targeted through schools, minimum income schemes, or local initiatives, so that resources are used efficiently and efforts match actual needs.

109682.explanation: The burden on residents must remain as low as possible. The municipality must remain critical of its own expenditures before raising taxes.

109685.explanation: Expansion of business parks can contribute to the local economy and employment, provided it is done carefully and does not compromise livability.

109687.explanation: For scarce social rental homes, priority should be given to local residents so that Barendrechters no longer miss out.

109689.explanation: Let's first make use of the roofs. The amount of agricultural land in Barendrecht is very limited anyway.

109690.explanation: The facility of BVV Barendrecht is very outdated and unsafe. Renewal is necessary but primarily a responsibility of the club. The adopted council proposal is flawed and amounts to a disguised tax increase of approximately 140 euros per resident. What about other clubs?

109691.explanation: Swimming skills are important, but structural municipal funding for school swimming is not a core task. Schools and parents can take responsibility here themselves, possibly supported through regional collaborations or sports clubs.

109694.explanation: True high-rise buildings do not fit Barendrecht. Higher construction increases housing opportunities. By raising the residential standard from four to five floors and up to seven floors on a project basis, we increase housing opportunities without losing the village character.

109696.explanation: There is no room for a community center in every neighborhood. However, there should be more to do and experience for young and old in Barendrecht, tailored to the needs of each neighborhood.

109700.explanation: A mosque changes the identity of Barendrecht. There are concerns about social isolation, nuisance, deterioration, and unclear financing. Many residents chose Barendrecht precisely to avoid Rotterdam-like situations, partly due to cultural tensions with Dutch values and norms.

109702.explanation: We doubt whether young people will commit to meeting places. Encounters are possible at many locations.

109643.explanation: We value diversity, encourage it, and want to facilitate it. This does not mean that diversity must be forced and that availability and quality of candidates must be decisive.

109645.explanation: This way residents feel a sense of ownership.

109647.explanation: Some hospitality businesses need tailored solutions. We support this.

109648.explanation: The municipality should encourage such initiatives in view of grid congestion and sustainability goals.

109650.explanation: Barendrecht must comply with national agreements.

109652.explanation: We take no position on this.

109654.explanation: This creates more togetherness and reduces fireworks nuisance and vandalism.

109657.explanation: Sports should be accessible to everyone.

109662.explanation: Safety is an important asset. Through the BOAs, the municipality can achieve this, among other things.

109663.explanation: Camera surveillance is good, but not all of Barendrecht should be covered with cameras.

109664.explanation: Femicide and violence against women are unacceptable. The municipality must be able to help women in this situation.

109665.explanation: We find this unnecessary; only the police may use force.

109666.explanation: This way, starters in Barendrecht have a better chance of getting a home in Barendrecht.

109670.explanation: The housing shortage is urgent, and this is a way to address it.

109672.explanation: In this way, we want to ensure that people continue to have day activities and remain active in society.

109675.explanation: The municipality can best do this through tailored solutions. It is NOT mandatory.

109677.explanation: Parking should remain free throughout Barendrecht.

109678.explanation: Barendrecht is too small for such an environmental zone.

109679.explanation: Care is best and most effective when it is also inclusive and takes different contexts into account.

109681.explanation: In a prosperous municipality like Barendrecht, we consider this unnecessary.

109682.explanation: The Property Tax (OZB) should not be a cash cow or be used to fix financial mistakes of the municipality.

109685.explanation: Building land in Barendrecht is scarce. Barendrecht should primarily focus on housing.

109687.explanation: The municipality must adhere to agreements made with cooperation partners.

109689.explanation: No active policy. If farmers want this, it should be possible.

109690.explanation: The municipality can assist, but the main responsibility lies with BVV itself.

109691.explanation: Swimming is important in a water-rich country like the Netherlands. Children must at least obtain swimming diploma A.

109694.explanation: Given the limited building land, high-rise buildings are necessary in the Barendrecht situation.

109696.explanation: This way, community cohesion in the neighborhoods is strengthened and people can get to know each other.

109700.explanation: Every religious community in Barendrecht deserves its own place of worship.

109702.explanation: Young people deserve their own place for meeting and relaxation.

statements.title: There should be more meeting places for young people in Barendrecht.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Barendrecht should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
There should be more camera surveillance in Barendrecht.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) in Barendrecht.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Barendrecht should introduce a parking permit scheme in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Barendrecht moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The business parks in Barendrecht should be allowed to expand.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Barendrecht should renovate BVV Barendrecht.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Barendrecht.
Barendrecht should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Barendrecht.

titleParts.text: In Barendrecht moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who patrol for safety in Barendrecht should be given batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Barendrecht controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a mosque in Barendrecht.
